name: Black Bean Chili (Prodigy)
recipeIngredient: 1 Chile negro or ancho chile, for chili powder
3 medium Yellow onions, diced into 1/4-inch squares
1 1/2 pounds Ripe or canned tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped, juice reserved
2 Poblano or Anaheim, roasted, peeled and diced
2 ounces Canned green chiles, rinsed well and diced
1/2 cup Creme fraiche or sour cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort through the beans and remove any small stones.
2. Rinse them well, cover them generously with water, and let them soak overnight.
3. Drain the beans, cover them with fresh water by a couple of inches, and bring to a boil with the bay leaf.
4. Lower the heat and let the beans simmer while you prepare the rest of the ingredients.
5. Heat a small heavy skillet over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds; when they begin to color, add the oregano leaves, shaking the pan frequently so the herbs don't scorch.
6. As soon as the fragrance is strong and robust, remove the pan from the heat and add the paprika and the cayenne. Give everything a quick stir; then remove from the pan—the paprika and the cayenne only need a few seconds to toast.
7. Grind in a mortar or spice mill to make a coarse powder.
8. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
9. To make the chili powder, put the dried chile in the oven for 3-to-5 minutes to dry it out. Cool briefly; then remove the stem, seeds and veins. Tear the pod into small pieces and grind into a powder in a blender or spice mill.
10. Heat oil in a large skillet and sauté onions over medium heat until they soften.
11. Add garlic, salt, and ground herbs and chili powder and cook another 5 minutes.
12. Add tomatoes and their juice. Simmer everything together for 15 minutes; then add this mixture to the beans, and if necessary, enough water so the beans are covered by at least 1-inch.
13. Continue cooking the beans slowly until they are soft, an hour or longer, or pressure cook them for 30 minutes at 15 pounds pressure. Keep an eye on the water level and add more, if needed, to keep the beans amply covered.
14. When the beans are cooked, taste and season with vinegar, additional salt if needed, and chopped cilantro.
15. Prepare garnishes: roast fresh green chiles over flame until evenly charred; let steam 10 minutes in a bowl covered with a dish; scrape off skins, discard seeds, and dice.
16. Serve chili ladled over a large spoonful of grated cheese and garnish with creme fraiche or sour cream, green chilies, and a sprig of fresh cilantro. This thick chili is best served alongside fritters or cornbread but can also be thinned considerably with stock, water, or tomato juice to make a flavorful black bean soup.
